promo_headline: Quiet Sunday. Three essays, two model updates.
whats_news: Every: a long essay on workspace agents
Platformer: weekly recap on labs vs platforms
Yann LeCun: a post about world-model evals
AI Tidbits: small-team deployment of frontier models

digest.title: A reading Sunday — fewer pings, deeper takes.
digest.body: Sunday is essay-shaped. Every and Platformer publish their long pieces, Google nudges Gemini on-device speed, Yann LeCun pushes back on benchmark theater, and AI Tidbits explains how small teams now ship frontier-class models. Nothing in the day demands action; the cost is missing the read.

items.headline: Every: the workspace agent essay
items.deck: Every published a long piece on what a workspace-level agent does that an in-app one cannot. This matters because the workspace surface is the real product battle of the next two quarters.
items.image_alt: "The Concourse of the Birds", Folio 11r from a Mantiq al-Tayr (Language of the Birds) — Habiballah of Sava (ca. 1600)

items.headline: Platformer weekly: labs vs platforms
items.deck: Casey Newton frames the labs-vs-platforms tension that will define the next regulatory cycle. This matters because the framing leaks into every panel discussion you sit through this month.
items.image_alt: ”Abiding nowhere, the awakened mind arises” — Musō Soseki (early to mid-14th century)

items.headline: Google: Gemini gets faster on-device
items.deck: Google rolled a kernel update that drops Gemini Nano latency on Pixel devices. This matters because device-side latency is the gate for ambient AI features going mainstream.

items.headline: Yann LeCun pushes back on benchmark theater
items.deck: Yann LeCun posted a long thread arguing world-model evals are more honest than current LLM benchmarks. This matters because benchmark talk is how labs justify their next round and the framing is shifting underfoot.
items.image_alt: Ia Orana Maria (Hail Mary) — Paul Gauguin (1891)

items.headline: AI Tidbits: 4-person teams running frontier models
items.deck: AI Tidbits walks through a real four-person team that deploys 70B+ models in production. This matters because the small-team-frontier story changes hiring expectations for early-stage builders.
items.image_alt: "The Concourse of the Birds", Folio 11r from a Mantiq al-Tayr (Language of the Birds) — Habiballah of Sava (ca. 1600)

items.headline: Read the Every essay end to end. No skim.
items.why: Sunday is the only day you have an uninterrupted hour. Skimming wastes the essay.
items.try: Set a 45-min timer. Sit with coffee, read once through, then re-read the section that argues with you.
items.image_alt: "Shah Jahan on Horseback", Folio from the Shah Jahan Album — Payag (verso: ca. 1630; recto: ca. 1530–50)

items.headline: Note one belief you'd change after Platformer + Every.
items.why: Two essays in one morning is rare; capture the shift before the week erases it.
items.try: Set a 15-min timer. Write one sentence: "Last week I thought X. Now I think Y."
items.image_alt: Ten Rakan Examining a Painting of White-Robed Kannon — Katō Nobukiyo (1788–92)
